zh.lifeSummary: Amie Gaveston是Piers Gaveston 1st Earl of Cornwall和unidentified mistress的女兒，於1312年1月6日之後出生在英國肯特托布利其。她於1334年與John de Driby在英國越可爾衛克菲德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1340年6月12日之後在英國越可爾衛克菲德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Amie Gaveston是Piers Gaveston 1st Earl of Cornwall和unidentified mistress的女儿，于1312年1月6日之后出生在英国肯特托布利其。她于1334年与John de Driby在英国越可爾衛克菲德结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1340年6月12日之后在英国越可爾衛克菲德。

nameMeanings.name: Gaveston
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: probably an altered form of   Graveson  , hypercorrecting the process seen in   Urmson   from Urmston, Lancs.

variants.name: Graveson
meanings: English: relationship name, ‘son of the steward, farm bailiff, or village headman’, largely confined to northern England. From the north Midlands through Yorks to the north-west, the source is Middle English greyve, grayve, greve, a borrowing of Old Scandinavian greifi, see Grave . In Durham and Northumb the source is Middle English greve, from Old (Northumbrian) English grǣfa, see Grieve . The two forms are difficult to tell apart, except that the post-medieval spelling Grieve- usually reflects the Northumbrian term. Pronunciation developments have sometimes obscured the origin of the name. Grayveson frequently lost the -v-, becoming Gra(y)son and Greeson; see Grayson .

variants.name: Grayson
meanings: English (northern): variant of Graveson, ‘son of the steward, farm bailiff, or village headman’. From the North Midlands through Yorkshire to the north-west, the source is Middle English grayve, grayve, greve, a borrowing of Old Norse greifi ‘earl, count’ (see Grave ). In Durham and Northumberland the source is Middle English greve, from Old (Northumbrian) English grǣfa with the same meaning (see Grieve ).
English: possibly a habitational name from Greysouthen (Cumbria), recorded as Graysone in 1505.

nameMeanings.name: Amie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of   Amy  , corresponding in form to the French vocabulary word meaning ‘(female) friend’.

variants.name: Amy
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Amie Gaveston was born after 6 January 1312, in Tonbridge, Kent, England as the daughter of Piers Gaveston 1st Earl of Cornwall and unidentified mistress. She married John de Driby in 1334, in Wakefield, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died after 12 June 1340, in Wakefield, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Amie Gaveston est née après 6 janvier 1312, à Tonbridge, Kent, Angleterre, fille de Piers Gaveston 1st Earl of Cornwall et unidentified mistress. Elle a épousé John de Driby en 1334, à Wakefield, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée après 12 juin 1340, à Wakefield, Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Amie Gaveston wurde nach 6. Januar 1312 in Tonbridge, Kent, England, als Tochter von Piers Gaveston 1st Earl of Cornwall und unidentified mistress geboren. ja.lifeSummary: Amie Gavestonは1312年1月6日以後にイギリスケントトンブリッジでPiers Gaveston 1st Earl of Cornwallとunidentified mistressの娘として生まれた。1334年にイギリスヨークシュアイアワケフィールドでJohn de Dribyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1340年6月12日以後にイギリスヨークシュアイアワケフィールドで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Amie Gaveston(은)는 1312년 1월 6일 이후 영국 켄트 토브리지에서 Piers Gaveston 1st Earl of Cornwall(와)과 unidentified mistress의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1334년 영국 요르크실르 웨이크필드에서 John de Driby(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1340년 6월 12일 이후 영국 요르크실르 웨이크필드에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Amie Gaveston nasceu após 6 de janeiro de 1312, em Tonbridge, Kent, Inglaterra filha de Piers Gaveston 1st Earl of Cornwall e unidentified mistress. Ela casou-se com John de Driby em 1334, em Wakefield, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu após 12 de junho de 1340, em Wakefield, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Amie Gaveston родилась после 6 январь 1312 г. в местности тонбридж, кэнт, Англия. Её родителей звали Piers Gaveston 1st Earl of Cornwall и unidentified mistress. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John de Driby в 1334 г. в местности веикефиелд, еркшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла после 12 июнь 1340 г. в местности веикефиелд, еркшир, Англия.
